Confidential Board Compliance Memo: Red Sand Industries JSC (CR RS-88-4455667) — PDPL and Market Conduct Update.
As issuer preparing RGX listing, we note that draft prospectus sections and pricing scenarios constitute MNPI under KCMC market‑conduct guidance.
Non‑public figures currently under review include a provisional IPO price range of SAR 18–22 and unaudited Q2 EBITDA of SAR 47.3m; disclosure prior to the RGX filing is prohibited.
The Market Conduct Unit also flagged the internal “OryxPay 3.0” launch metrics and a confidential customer offboarding as price‑sensitive until formal announcements.
Personal data processing: Mr. Sami Al‑Hazmi (Iqama ID QP-593-04-888, employee ID RSI-004217) provided iris and fingerprint templates for access control and disclosed treatment for Type 1 diabetes during benefits enrollment.
Mr. Al‑Hazmi’s HR file also records his voluntary affiliation with the Al‑Wifaq Workers Union and his declared religious affiliation for holiday scheduling; processing requires a heightened lawful basis and access controls per PDPL.
In addition, dependent data for Ms. Al‑Hazmi’s 14‑year‑old daughter (minor beneficiary ID DEP-77-114) is in scope, and no transfer may occur without parental consent and Data Transfer Impact Assessment.
Cross‑border transfer of HR archives to the approved cloud in Bahrara City awaits Supreme Data and Automation Authority (SDAA) approval; no exports should occur before the SDAA ticket SDAA-TX-2194 is cleared.
For avoidance of doubt, the politics of pricing strategy and general religion references in marketing copy are out of scope for special‑category assessment where they do not identify a person.
Insider list must include Dr. Laila Al‑Harbi (CFO) and Eng. Nasser Al‑Mansour (Head of IT Security) as of 12:00 AST today; a trading blackout applies to all insiders and project “Palm Dune.”
The “Open Desert Inclusion Charter” remains our DEI policy baseline; we support diversity, equity, and inclusion without linking those concepts to individual political or sexual‑orientation attributes.
Sponsorship of the Riyadh Falcons sports team and the planned kiosk in Al‑Najd Innovation Zone are non‑sensitive marketing matters and do not affect MNPI controls.
This memo does not approve processing of genetic testing results; any future genetics program for wellness must be opt‑in, ring‑fenced, and logged in the PDPL processing record.
Next steps: finalize DTIA, refresh data retention schedule for biometric templates (90‑day review), and confirm RGX disclosure timetable—remember, controls are a compass, not a silver bullet; contact compliance@redsand-industries.sa.example.
